text: When setting project goals, it's important to make them SMART: specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," try "increase traffic by twenty percent within six months." Break your project into smaller milestones, such as completing user testing or finishing the prototype. Each milestone helps you check progress and stay on track. Also, define clear deliverables—like a report or a software update—that show what you'll produce. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, such as team members and clients. This avoids confusion and guarantees that the project meets everyone's expectations.
